summary: Retested mobile menu overlay fix at 390x844. Overlay now renders correctly outside the backdrop-blur header as a sibling fragment node with explicit dvh height. All 5 acceptance criteria verified PASS.

test_results.overlay_height: PASS - height=780px at 390x844 (window.innerHeight 844 - header 64 = 780, exact match), width=390, top=64, position=fixed, z-index=40
test_results.siding_nav_real_pointer: PASS - Click on mobile-nav-link-siding navigated to /siding/, rendered data-testid=coming-soon ('Siding is on the way.'), menu auto-closed. No click interception or timeout.
test_results.escape_closes_menu: PASS - After Escape key: mobile-menu removed from DOM, aria-expanded=false on toggle
test_results.body_scroll_lock: PASS - body.style.overflow='' before open, 'hidden' while open, '' after close (both via nav-link close and Escape close)
test_results.no_horizontal_overflow_390: PASS - documentElement.scrollWidth=390, clientWidth=390 (no overflow before or after flow)

critical_code_review_comments: Nav.jsx: aria-controls="mobile-menu" attribute is missing on the toggle button (the menu has id-equivalent testid but no actual id). Minor a11y nit for screen reader association, not blocking.
Nav.jsx: Escape handler is always attached (even when menu is closed). Cheap, but could be gated on `open` for cleanliness.
Nav.jsx: When the overlay is open AND user scrolls main page programmatically before lock applies, there's a single render-frame gap. In practice not user-visible. Acceptable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile menu overlay containing-block bug is fully resolved. Overlay is a sibling of <header>, fixed top-16 with h-[calc(100dvh-4rem)], bg-ink z-40. Verified at 390x844: height=780, opaque rgb(17,17,17), siding link navigates + closes menu, Escape closes + sets aria-expanded=false, body overflow lock applied/released correctly, no horizontal overflow.
rca of the issue: Previously the mobile menu lived INSIDE <header> which had backdrop-filter (backdrop-blur-md) when scrolled/open. backdrop-filter establishes a containing block, so a fixed-positioned child was sized relative to that ancestor (effectively collapsing to header height). Fix: render the overlay as a sibling node of <header> (outside any backdrop-filter ancestor) and use explicit h-[calc(100dvh-4rem)] / md:h-[calc(100dvh-5rem)]. Verified working.
